trivia_bullets.text: Vial of Poison was printed in Magic 2014 (M14), released July 19, 2013 — one of the final standalone core sets before Wizards shifted the release cadence toward block-only releases. M14 introduced planeswalker-themed marketing but otherwise retained the core set's function as an accessible entry point. [Scryfall]

The card costs {1} to cast and another {1} to activate, then sacrifices itself to grant any creature deathtouch until end of turn. Colorless access to instant-speed deathtouch regardless of deck color. EDHREC rank 23,249 reflects narrow single-use design. [Scryfall, EDHREC]
"There are worse ways to die, but not many." — unattributed [Scryfall]. Ambient M14 flavor voice; pithy memento mori from the victim's perspective.

Artist Franz Vohwinkel has illustrated approximately 134 MTG cards [Gatherer via WebSearch]. His chiaroscuro still-life rendering echoes Dutch vanitas painting — a genre built on poison, skulls, and decaying luxury objects.

related_cards.name: Virulent Swipe
related_cards.note: instant-speed deathtouch grant, direct functional parallel

related_cards.name: Coat with Venom
related_cards.note: same effect as a common instant
